summary: High‑quality audience tape of the spring ’91 Omni show, delivering a fun vibe with standout instrumental Stir It Up and strong renditions of Queen Jane Approximately, The Other One, Wharf Rat and Quinn.
review: Both reviewers praise this audience recording for its immersive "you are there" feel despite the notoriously tricky acoustics of the Omni. The performance is described as solid and possibly one of the best of the tour, highlighted by an instrumental Stir It Up and strong keyboard work throughout several songs. A brief audio hiccup at the very start of Shakedown Street is noted but does not detract from the overall enjoyment.

ai_rating.rationale: Two consistent 4‑star reviews highlight a good show with several standout songs and an audience recording that is judged high quality despite minor initial audio issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, immersive "you are there" feel; minor audio issue at the start of Shakedown Street that disappears quickly. No major drop‑outs or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Described as a fun, spring‑time three‑night run with solid energy throughout; the set is considered one of the better performances of the 1991 tour.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard work on the five‑minute instrumental Stir It Up was highlighted as a key factor in making the performance sweet.
